summary: Set 094 Session 2 (set-terminal) — the onboarding/lifecycle half of the Work Explorer module-first redesign (091-094), and the single-release-boundary prep. (1) D6 module-decomposition copy-prompt: a new dabbler.copyModuleDecompositionPrompt command AND a 'Copy AI decomposition prompt' Define-modules form button (copy-decomposition-prompt gettingStartedAction) share one flow (runCopyModuleDecompositionPromptFlow) — ensureModulesManifest runs FIRST (the FOURTH explicit-action ensure-write site; adjudication A — never a passive/activation/render path), then a pointer-style prompt (references docs/modules.yaml + the project plan, states the invariants: globally-unique set names, module = grouping-not-identity) is copied. Referenced from the manifest header comment, never embedded. (2) Parallel-sets UI shelved with an escape hatch (routed ruling 1a, s2-parallel-and-d6-architecture.json): dabbler.generateParallelSessionSetPrompt is now the sole live feeder of PARALLEL_GUIDANCE (chosen over a sticky parallelHint setting that would contaminate the common decomposition path); the prerequisites: machinery + worktree tooling are untouched, regression-pinned. (3) Docs pass: the Q8 compat matrix marked shipped-state; quick-start + both READMEs updated for the two-section form; the 092 'Session Set Explorer' -> 'Work Explorer' rename residue swept across the live docs (20 renames; 2 historical release-record entries preserved). (4) Release prep: extension bumped 0.41.0 -> 0.42.0 as the single 091-094 release-boundary VSIX; omnibus CHANGELOG entry covering Sets 087 + 091-094, superseding the never-published 0.41.0 (Set 086 folds in); publish is operator-gated (tag vsix-v0.42.0; the workflow verifies the tag vs package.json; extension-only, no coordinated router bump). Suite green: extension unit 1487, Playwright 26 (incl. the two-section + copy-decomposition button smoke and the never-write-on-activation E2E), pytest 2922/6 (unchanged — zero Python touched), tsc clean. Advisory path-aware critique VERIFIED by BOTH critics (gpt-5.4 + gemini-2.5-pro), zero findings — each read the code directly and confirmed the load-bearing claims. DEFERRED to Set 095 (verdict-assigned onboarding rewrite): the getting-started.md.template teaching-doc rewrite + onboarding screenshot refresh + the comprehensive UX UAT re-cut — recorded pre-publish caveats (CHANGELOG 0.42.0 note). UAT (requiresUAT: suggested, non-gating): the 094 surface is Layer-2/3 covered; 092's human walks are armed pending the operator; pre-publish UAT is operator-adjudicated.
verification_adjudication: Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-6, anthropic excluded) ran two rounds; R1 and R2 each surfaced exactly one Major on the SHIPPING extension README's onboarding sequence — it described a circular first-run path (the per-module row actions create the first session set, yet the tree carrying those rows only appears once >=1 set exists). Fixed in flight: traced selectExplorerMode (form while zero sets -> the Command Palette 'Import Project Plan' + 'Generate Session-Set Prompt' create the first plan + set -> the tree with per-module row actions appears once >=1 set exists) and rewrote the README to that actual behavior — directly implementing the verifier's own prescribed 'correct answer'. At the 2-round automatic cap (Set 086 operator rule) the operator ADJUDICATED the fix as resolving the Major (AskUserQuestion: 'Accept fix, close adjudicated'), declining a confirming round 3. Closed via the sanctioned attested --manual-verify override (reason file). The immutable raw artifacts (s2-verification*.md / s2-issues*.json) preserve the full ISSUES_FOUND history.
